task: I’m putting together a small training handout and want to make sure I don’t miss anything important before I send it out. Can you help me with a quick pre-send checklist for the materials and any backups I should keep on hand?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a quick pre-send checklist you can use for a training handout and backups.

## Pre-send checklist
- [ ] Handout title matches the session title
- [ ] Date, time, and location/link are correct
- [ ] Audience name/group is correct
- [ ] All names, roles, and contact info are accurate
- [ ] Content matches the latest version of the session
- [ ] No missing pages or sections
- [ ] Links work
- [ ] Images, charts, and tables display correctly
- [ ] Formatting is clean and readable
- [ ] File name is clear and final
- [ ] Spelling and grammar checked
- [ ] Version number/date added if you use them
- [ ] Accessibility checked if needed
- [ ] You’ve confirmed who should receive it

## Backups to keep on hand
- [ ] One printed copy for yourself
- [ ] One extra printed copy for the room
- [ ] PDF version saved separately
- [ ] Editable version saved in case of changes
- [ ] A plain-text or simplified version if needed
- [ ] Spare slides or notes if the handout is tied to a presentation
- [ ] Contact list for quick follow-up if corrections are needed

## Final quick check before sending
- [ ] Open the file one last time
- [ ] Scan for anything that would confuse a first-time attendee
- [ ] Make sure you can answer: “If this file doesn’t open, what’s my backup?”

If you want, I can turn this into a one-page printable version you can keep with the handout.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this works. It hits the main stuff I actually worry about — version, links, backups, readable formatting, and having a hard copy. The extra check at the end is good too.

I’d probably keep:
- final PDF
- editable file
- printed copy
- any notes or attendance sheet if it goes with the session
- a backup in another folder or drive, not just one place

That’s enough for me.
